name: Pizza
description: From Naples to NYC — the history, styles, and trivia of the world's favorite food.

questions.q: What is the most popular pizza topping in the U.S.?

source.notes: Featured on ~36% of U.S. pizzas.

questions.q: Which Italian city is the birthplace of modern pizza?

source.notes: Pizza Napoletana has UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage status (2017).

questions.q: Which Italian queen is the Margherita pizza named after?

source.notes: Created in 1889 by pizzaiolo Raffaele Esposito; tomato/mozzarella/basil represent the Italian flag.

questions.q: What three colors of the Italian flag are represented by the toppings on a Margherita pizza?

source.notes: Tomato (red), mozzarella (white), basil (green).

questions.q: Which is widely considered the first U.S. pizzeria, opened in 1905?

source.notes: Lombardi's Pizza in Manhattan.

questions.q: Which American city is famous for thick, deep-dish pizza?

source.notes: Pizzeria Uno is credited with inventing it in 1943.

questions.q: What style of pizza is square-cut, thick-crusted, and made on auto-shop pans?

source.notes: Buddy's Rendezvous in Detroit invented it in 1946 using auto-parts trays.

questions.q: What is the Italian word for the folded pizza turnover?

source.notes: Italian for 'trouser leg.'

questions.q: Which classic Italian pizza is topped only with tomato, garlic, oregano, and olive oil — NO cheese?

source.notes: Despite the name, contains no seafood — 'marinara' refers to seafarers.

questions.q: Which Italian pizza has four 'seasons' represented in each quarter (artichokes, ham, olives, mushrooms)?

source.notes: Spring (artichokes), Summer (basil + tomato), Autumn (mushrooms), Winter (ham).

questions.q: What is 'Quattro Formaggi' pizza topped with?

source.notes: Typically mozzarella, gorgonzola, parmesan, and another (e.g. fontina).

questions.q: Which controversial topping was created in Canada in 1962?

source.notes: Created by Sam Panopoulos at Satellite Restaurant in Chatham, Ontario.

questions.q: What month is U.S. National Pizza Month?

source.notes: Established by Gerry Durnell in 1984.

questions.q: Which pizza chain is the largest in the world by revenue?

source.notes: Domino's surpassed Pizza Hut in 2017.

questions.q: Where was Pizza Hut founded in 1958?

source.notes: Founded by brothers Dan and Frank Carney.

questions.q: Where was Domino's Pizza founded in 1960?

source.notes: Founded by Tom Monaghan as 'DomiNick's'.

questions.q: What is the Italian-American 'pizza pie' style with a thin, flexible crust folded for eating?

source.notes: Large slices folded for eating on the go.

questions.q: Approximately how many slices of pizza are eaten per second in the United States?

source.notes: About 350 slices per second / 3 billion pizzas per year in the U.S.

questions.q: Which St. Louis pizza style is famous for its cracker-thin crust and Provel cheese?

source.notes: Provel is a processed cheese blend used almost exclusively on St. Louis pizza.

questions.q: Which California chef popularized California-style pizza in the 1980s?

source.notes: Wolfgang Puck's Spago in 1982. Ed LaDou is also credited.
